lines: Before you think about what you need to do today... [short pause] [short pause] let's start with who you are.

Breathe with me. [short pause] In through your nose for four... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven. [short pause] And exhale slowly through your mouth... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven. [short pause] Exhale... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

One more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven. [short pause] And release... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

Good. [short pause] Feel your body settle. [short pause] Notice the ground beneath you... [short pause] [short pause] the surface supporting your weight. [short pause] You don't have to hold yourself up right now. [short pause] You're held.
Here's Paul, writing to the church in Ephesus:
"For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do." Ephesians 2, verse 10.

Handiwork. [short pause] The Greek word is poiema... [short pause] [short pause] it's where we get the English word "poem." You are not a product. [short pause] Not an output. [short pause] A poem. [short pause] Something crafted with intention, shaped with care, meant to say something that nothing else says.

And then there's Jeremiah 29:11... [short pause] [short pause] a verse you've probably seen on coffee mugs and dorm room posters. [short pause] "For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future."

Here's what the coffee mugs leave out. [short pause] God spoke those words to an entire nation living in exile. [short pause] In Babylon. [short pause] Displaced, defeated, grieving. [short pause] They'd lost their homes, their temple, everything familiar. [short pause] And God said: even here... [short pause] [short pause] I have plans. [short pause] Not after exile. [short pause] During exile. [short pause] The promise isn't that life will be comfortable. [short pause] It's that God is working purpose even in displacement.

So if your life doesn't look the way you thought it would... [short pause] [short pause] if you feel like you're in a season of exile, or waiting, or confusion... [short pause] [short pause] that doesn't disqualify you from purpose. [short pause] Jeremiah's audience was living proof.

Now let's bring this into your body. [short pause] Press your fingertips together... [short pause] [short pause] all ten, like a tent. [short pause] Feel the pressure points. [short pause] Each finger alive, sensitive, capable. [short pause] These hands will do something today. [short pause] They'll type, or hold, or build, or comfort, or create. [short pause] That's not nothing. [short pause] That's the good work Ephesians is talking about.

Purpose isn't always a grand calling. [short pause] Most days, it's small and specific. [short pause] The way you listen when someone needs to be heard. [short pause] The care you bring to work that no one notices. [short pause] The patience you offer when it costs you something. [short pause] The meal you make. [short pause] The message you send. [short pause] The standard you hold.

Let's sit with this gently. [short pause] Not "What is my one cosmic purpose?"... [short pause] [short pause] that question is too big for a Tuesday morning. [short pause] Instead: what good work is right in front of me today? [short pause] Who might I serve? [short pause] Where might I bring something... [short pause] [short pause] even something small... [short pause] [short pause] that only I can bring?

Maybe you know. [short pause] Maybe it's already obvious... [short pause] [short pause] your job, your family, your craft. [short pause] Or maybe purpose today is quieter. [short pause] Being present. [short pause] Showing up honestly. [short pause] Not performing, just being faithful in the small things.

Now release your fingertips and rest your hands, palms up, on your lap or at your sides. [short pause] Open hands. [short pause] Ready to receive the work and ready to do it. [short pause] Not grasping. [short pause] Not striving. [short pause] Open.

There's a freedom in realizing that purpose isn't something you have to invent. [short pause] Ephesians says it was prepared in advance. [short pause] It's already there. [short pause] Your job isn't to create purpose out of thin air. [short pause] It's to show up with enough presence and willingness to recognize it when it's in front of you.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] I don't always feel purposeful. [short pause] Some mornings I feel more like a rough draft than a poem. [short pause] But you said you made me as handiwork... [short pause] [short pause] crafted, intentional, not accidental. [short pause] Help me see the good work you've prepared for today. [short pause] Not the grand plan. [short pause] Just today's work. [short pause] Give me eyes to notice it and hands willing to do it. [short pause] Even if it's small. [short pause] Even if no one sees. [short pause] Amen.
You are God's handiwork. [short pause] Created with intention. [short pause] Placed in this exact moment for a reason.
The good work prepared for you today is real. [short pause] It's waiting. [short pause] And you don't have to see the whole blueprint to do the next faithful thing.
Go... [short pause] [short pause] not to perform, but to be present. [short pause] The poem is still being written.
